mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term, as consumer spending is a major component of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods characterized by sustained positive month-over-month RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of unemployment spikes with recessions
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also rising, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle to combat inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:historical central bank responses to inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both CPIAUCSL annual change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: In an economic expansion, increasing housing starts (HOUST) signal strong economic confidence and future construction activity, boosting overall demand and supporting positive S&P 500 returns. This reflects robust investment and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 20-day returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) during periods of confirmed economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon as investor confidence rises.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of quarters following GDPC1 growth exceeding 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating a potential economic slowdown that would negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns due to reduced output.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO shows a 3-month average decline greater than 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signifies a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon due to increased economic activity.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for companies and encourages investment, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 returns by boosting corporate valuations.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative during periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 1% for more than two consecutive quarters.
